description: §20.8.1 — a single flat `<outlet>` inside a `<program>` shell is the canonical Client-Router swap region. It is recognized as a structural element (no E-STRUCTURAL-ELEMENT-MISPLACED), fires none of the E-OUTLET-* family, and emits a stable addressable region marker `<div data-scrml-outlet>` into the document body (the Wave-1b soft-nav swap target). The domAnchored half asserts exactly one `[data-scrml-outlet]` region exists in the live DOM.
rationale: §20.8.1: the shell MAY contain exactly one `<outlet>` — the region into which the current route's content renders. One flat outlet in a `<program>` is well-formed: no placement/duplicate error fires, and codegen emits the addressable `[data-scrml-outlet]` region.
